desc: Round-4 R4-A: a template `ref="box"` whose name ALSO names a `<data>` key (`$data.box`). Pre-fix react emitted two `const box` (state + `useRef`) → TS2451 dup + the `$refs.box` read even misbound to the state dep-mirror; svelte two `let box` (state + `bind:this`) → dup + ROZ621; angular two `box` class members (signal + viewChild) → silent TS2300; vue falsely errored ROZ420 even though its own output uses the distinct `boxRef`. Solid (`box$localRef`) + Lit (`_box`/`_refBox`) already prefix refs and were correct. Fix: R3-5's `deconflictRefsAgainstUserBindings` (react/svelte/angular) now triggers on state/computed/prop names too — renaming the INTERNAL ref to `<name>Ref`, never the declared primitive (author-facing $data/$props contract). Vue's Pitfall-4 check now tests the LOWERED `<name>Ref` binding (vue always suffixes refs), so `ref="box"` + `$data.box` no longer false-errors while a real `ref="box"` + `$data.boxRef` collision still fails loud. Found by the shape-matrix harness (react dup-decl + the new Angular duplicate-class-member invariant).
discoveredBy: shape-matrix torture harness (spike 012) — Round 4 (react dup-decl + angular dup-member invariant)
